rulings.Case Title: BERRY & BERRY, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION VS. GABRIELLE JACKSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Amend Third Amended Complt Against Deft Wells Fargo Bank
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 17, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF BERRY & BERRY, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION'S Motion To Amend Third Amended Complaint Against Defendant Wells Fargo Bank. Granted. For reasons referred to in connection with the motion for summary judgment, it appears plaintiff can state a cause of action for conversion. While the claimed recent discovery (July 3, 2012 Butzbaugh Deposition) may or may not add much to plaintiff's evidence, no prejudice to Wells Fargo appears and leave to amend should usually be granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: BERRY & BERRY, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION VS. GABRIELLE JACKSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 17, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. There is a triable issue whether certain funds were held in trust by Borrower J&W. See Marshall Dec. (filed July 27, 2012), Ex B, ex. 4 thereof (checks "in trust"). While it is true that the words 'in trust' alone "do[] not necessarily manifest an intent to create a trust," it is a circumstances to be considered. Petherbridge v. Prudential Sav. & Loan Assn., 79 Cal. App. 3d 509, 516-17 (1978)(emphasis supplied). On summary judgment I am required to make all reasonable inferences against the moving party, and I am not permitted to weigh the evidence. On the basis of this evidence I infer a trust. Those funds would not be subject to seizure by Wells Fargo as it sought to recoup against its line of credit. Re: Request for Judicial Notice: Not opposed and accordingly granted. Re: Notice of Lodging (Brumfield Declaration): there is no motion to seal and accordingly the lodged document will be filed in the public file. Judge Karnow discloses that he and his family have checking and savings accounts with Wells Fargo. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS WEISEL VS. RYAN ASSOCIATES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 17, 2012, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT WELCH MARBLE & TILE, INC.'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Motion To Dismiss. The motion to quash is granted. Cross-Complainant fails to establish that cross-defendant has minimum contacts with California to warrant personal jurisdiction or that cross-defendant consented to California jurisdiction through the subcontract, which incorporated the prime contract. There is nothing in the prime contract to indicate that the forum selection clause applies to subcontractors. The request for additional discovery on jurisdictional matters is denied because there is no showing of a reasonable possibility that relevant facts in support of jurisdiction are available. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE, PROCEEDING UNDER A PSEUDONYM VS. WELLS FARGO & COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 17, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO & COMPANY, WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment granted. Judge Karnow discloses that he and his family have checking and savings accounts with Wells Fargo. The undisputed facts show the following. Plaintiff voluntarily determined not to accept offers of a ride home from the Greenery (the only place in the narrative of this case which was sponsored by Wells Fargo or was otherwise work related). Plaintiff went to Artie's on her own, without instruction or invitation from any manager or supervisor of Wells, where she became voluntarily intoxicated. The events at Artie's had no connection with Wells Fargo or otherwise with employment. Plaintiff voluntarily left Artie's and went to Gretz's home, the site of the (alleged) assault. The assault did not take place within the course and scope of employment. The asserted violation of a workplace guidelines does not establish the employer's vicarious liability for the offending conduct. In any event the guidelines relate to company sponsored events, and nothing that happened at Artie's or at the site of the assault was company sponsored. Respondeat superior does not apply because the assault was not a foreseeable event inherent in the conduct of the workplace, including (for purposes of this motion) the Wells party at the Greenery, including the drinking there. Wells undertook no adverse employment action, having not taken an action such as demotion or a change in responsibilities. Wells is not responsible for constructive discharge because its acts (allowing Gretz to continue on in employment) were not unreasonable: Wells was not required (based on the information it had at the time) to terminate Gretz, and Wells ensured that plaintiff would not have to interact with Gretz. Later when Wells found out that Gretz and Folas had lied, they were terminated. At the hearing the parties must provide proposed orders on the evidentiary objections, one for all the objections of Wells and one for all the objections of plaintiff, which repeat the content of the previously filed Objections. Even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested, Wells should prepare an order granting the motion for summary judgment which includes the substantive portions of this tentative as well as the appropriate portions of the proposed order lodged May 24, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: MICHAEL COOMBS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Memo Of Costs/Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 17, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANT ARCHIBALD CUNNINGHAM'S Motion To Strike Memo Of Costs/Tax Costs. The motion is denied. The court may determine fees and costs even though an appeal on the merits may be pending. Fees are recoverable if incurred, and if as here the fees are authorized by contract and the underlying award includes an award of attorneys fees. The fees sought are reasonable, especially in the light of actions by A. Cunningham during the pendency of this litigation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: MUNICIPAL ATTORNEY'S ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 17, 2012, Line 9, PETITIONERS MUNICIPAL ATTORNEY'S ASSOCIATION and MARSANNE WEESE'S Petition To Compel Arbitration. Granted. The FAA does not apply because Petitioner failed to show that the MOU involves interstate commerce. The MOU contains a valid and enforceable arbitration agreement that covers the dispute raised by Petitioner. There was no waiver because at least some of the damages claimed (delay damages) post-date the signing of her release. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOWONNA BUTLER et al VS. EDGAR GARCIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 17, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANT EDGAR GARCIA and JANE CREAGER'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. The motion is denied. The principles of res judicata and collateral estoppel do not apply because the prior action and the instant action involve different causes of action and the re-letting issue was not previously adjudicated. The Ellis Act issue was not "necessarily decided" in the previous proceeding. rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Deft Sain Francis Memorial Hospital To Provide Further Resposnes To Pltf'S First Request For Production Of Documents And Produce All Requested Non-Priveleged Documents, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 17, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Deft Saint Francis Memorial Hospital To Provide Further Resposnes To Pltf'S First Request For Production Of Documents And Produce All Requested Non-Priveleged Documents, And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: ROSARIO M. O'CONNOR et al VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 17, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANT EAST WEST BANK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first, sixth and seventh causes of action. As to the first cause of action, common law negligence has been generally displaced by the California Commercial Code and the alleged facts do not fall into an exception. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege specific objective indicia of fraud that should have placed Defendant East West Bank on notice of impropriety. As to the sixth cause of action, Plaintiffs have alleged "serious" emotional distress which is usually adequate this stage. E.g., Wong v. Tai Jing, 189 Cal. App. 4th 1354, 1377 (2010), citing Molien v. Kaiser Foundation Hospitals, 27 Cal.3d 916 (1980). However the demurrer as to the underlying tort (negligence) is sustained with leave, and accordingly the demurrer to this cause of action too must be sustained with leave to amend, as this claim rises and falls with the first cause of action. As to the seventh cause of action, Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege facts showing they had an ownership interest in the relevant checks when they were deposited at Defendant East West Bank. Demurrers based on "uncertainty," "estoppel," and the statute of limitations are overruled. It appears moving party cannot determine at this stage whether the statute of limitations applies and on that basis suggests uncertainty. Discovery will cure this problem. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Judge Karnow discloses that he and his family maintain checking and saving accounts with Wells Fargo. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: ROSARIO M. O'CONNOR et al VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant East West Bank'S Motion To Change Venue From San Francisco County To Los Angeles
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 17, 2012, Line 12, Defendant East West Bank's Motion To Change Venue From San Francisco County To Los Angeles. Denied. Venue is proper because Defendant Wells Fargo Bank's principal place of business is in San Francisco County. Defendant East West Bank has failed in meeting its burden regarding the convenience of witnesses. The convenience of party witnesses is generally not material to a motion brought to change venue on the basis of forum non conveniens, and moving party does not explain how the convenience of the few non-party witnesses would be affected by maintaining the case in San Francisco. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Judge Karnow discloses that he and his family maintain checking and saving accounts with Wells Fargo. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: KIRSTEN COLEMAN VS. ELECTRO POP ROCKS, A PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendant Michael Beeson
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 17, 2012, Line 13, Plaintiff's Order To Show Cause To Defendant Michael Beeson. Denied. The motion is an improper motion for reconsideration: Plaintiff has failed to explain precisely what the new evidence is, and why the new evidence could not have been provided earlier. On the merits, the motion fails. Plaintiff does not show a probability of prevailing or that the balance of harms tips in her favor. Moreover, it appears that money damages would appropriately remedy any harm. The thrust of the motion is to seek a mandatory injunction (see Reply filed September 12, 2012 at 2 line 16 (management of partnership activities)), as to which the burden on plaintiff is heightened. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)
